figures.caption: Fig. 4: Representative balance trajectories on open floor. (a) Torso tilt and (b) base height versus time during a single push episode at four force levels. The red band marks the 100ms push interval at t=1.0 s. The tilt envelope decays exponentially with damping ratio that scales inversely with force; the base height dip grows monotonically from 1.5 cm at 100N to 6.5 cm at 250N and recovers within ∼ 1 s. Tilt remains well below the 45◦ fall threshold across the entire force sweep.

Fig. 5: Latent mode t-SNE (300 episodes, 6 force levels, 50 each). Top: colored by push force; low-force episodes cluster where Mode 3≈ 1, high-force episodes spread as Modes 1 and 2 gain mass. Bottom: the same embedding colored by outcome; failures concentrate in the single-mode cluster while successes span the multi-mode region.

Fig. 3: Representative open-floor recovery rollout. Frames: (0) pre-push stance; (1) impact with F applied for 100ms; (2) compensatory whole-body motion; (3) stabilizing + steady-state recovery phase.

Fig. 2: RECOVERFORMER architecture. A causal transformer encodes the 50-step observation history into et. The latent mode head predicts zt over K = 4 modes; the affordance head predicts ct ∈ [0, 1]Kc . Both feed the action decoder.

Fig. 1: RECOVERFORMER maintains balance through a lateral push on the Unitree G1. Mid-recovery snapshot 160ms after a 180N torso impulse. The transformer policy converts a 50-step history of proprioception and contact-region distances into 29-DoF joint targets at 50Hz.
